bullets.text: The US military has denied Iranian claims that its latest strikes hit civilian infrastructure, stating that the attacks were intended to degrade Iranian military capabilities.
Many Ukrainian soldiers have expressed outrage over the removal of the defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, with protests erupting and troops criticising the decision.
A second tanker in three months has been hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates, indicating a potential resurgence of piracy in the region.
Universities in Australia have apologised to Jewish staff and students following an antisemitism inquiry that highlighted distress caused by protest movements on campuses.
One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has declined to disclose who funded her recent trip to Italy, where she attended a fashion show and stayed at a five-star resort.
A robodebt whistleblower alleges she was warned that her royal commission evidence could lead to job loss, according to court hearings, though her department denies these claims.
US chip stocks experienced their worst week in over a year, with high-flying semiconductor shares falling due to concerns that AI spending may not justify current valuations.
Emerging-market stocks have slumped into a technical correction, fueled by mounting concerns over the inflated valuations within the artificial intelligence industry.
China's trade gap is unexpectedly narrowing, as the world's second-biggest economy faces an unplanned shift towards fiscal austerity.
